name: pycrdt
documentation:
[![Build Status](https://github.com/jupyter-server/pycrdt/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?query=branch%3Amain++)](https://github.com/jupyter-server/pycrdt/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?query=branch%3Amain++)
[![Code Coverage](https://img.shields.io/badge/coverage-100%25-green)](https://img.shields.io/badge/coverage-100%25-green)

# pycrdt

CRDTs based on [Yrs](https://github.com/y-crdt/y-crdt/tree/main/yrs).

Documentation is available [here](https://jupyter-server.github.io/pycrdt).

classes.name: python.pycrdt._transaction.Transaction

methods.name: python.pycrdt._transaction.Transaction.origin
documentation:
The origin of the transaction.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: No current transaction.

A read-write transaction that can be used to mutate a document.
It must be used with a context manager (see [Doc.transaction()][pycrdt.Doc.transaction]):
```py
with doc.transaction():
    ...
```

classes.name: python.pycrdt._transaction.NewTransaction
documentation:
A read-write transaction that can be used to mutate a document.
It can be used with a context manager or an async context manager
(see [Doc.new_transaction()][pycrdt.Doc.new_transaction]):
```py
with doc.new_transaction():
    ...

async with doc.new_transaction():
    ...
```

classes.name: python.pycrdt._transaction.ReadTransaction
documentation: A read-only transaction that cannot be used to mutate a document.

Args:

documentation: The client states.

documentation: Starts updating the awareness periodically.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.stop
documentation: Stops updating the awareness periodically.

documentation:
Returns:

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.set_local_state

Updates the local state and meta, and sends the changes to subscribers.

Args:

documentation:
Sets a local state field.

Args:

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.remove_awareness_states
documentation:
Removes awareness states for clients given by their IDs.

Args:
    client_ids: The list of client IDs for which to remove the awareness states.
    origin: The origin of the update.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.encode_awareness_update
documentation:
Creates an encoded awareness update of the clients given by their IDs.

Args:
    client_ids: The list of client IDs for which to create an update.

Returns:

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.apply_awareness_update
documentation:
Applies the binary update and notifies subscribers with changes.

Args:

    origin: The origin of the update.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.observe
documentation:
Registers the given callback to awareness changes.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the awareness changes.

Returns:

methods.name: python.pycrdt._awareness.Awareness.unobserve
documentation:
Unregisters the given subscription ID from awareness changes.

Args:

classes.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__init__
documentation:
Creates a map with an optional initial value:
```py
map0 = Map()
map1 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 3, "baz": map0})
```

Args:
    init: The list from which to initialize the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__len__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 1})
assert len(map0) == 2
```
Returns:
    The length of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__str__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 1})
assert str(map0) == '{"foo":0,"bar":1}'
```

Returns:
    The string representation of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.to_py
documentation:
Recursively converts the map's items to Python objects, and
returns them in a `dict`. If the map was not yet inserted in a document,
returns `None` if the map was not initialized.

Returns:
    The map recursively converted to Python objects, or `None`.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__delitem__
documentation:
Removes the item at the given key from the map:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 1})
del map0["foo"]
assert map0.to_py() == {"bar": 1}
```

Args:
    key: The key of the item to remove.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the value at the given key:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 1})
assert map0["foo"] == 0
```

Returns:
    The value at the given key.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__setitem__
documentation:
Sets a value at the given key:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 1})
map0["foo"] = 2
assert map0["foo"] == 2
```

Args:
    key: The key to set.
    value: The value to set.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Key must be of type string.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__iter__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 0})
for key in m0:
    assert map[key] == 0
```
Returns:
    An iterable over the keys of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.__contains__
documentation:
Checks if the given key is in the map:
```py
Doc()["map0"] = map0 = Map({"foo": 0, "bar": 0})
assert "baz" not in map0:
```

Args:
    item: The key to look for in the map.

Returns:
    True if the key was found.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.get
documentation:
Returns the value corresponding to the given key if it exists, otherwise
returns the `default_value`.

Args:
    key: The key of the value to get.
    default_value: The optional default value to return if the key is not found.

Returns:
    The value at the given key, or the default value.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.pop
documentation:
Removes the entry at the given key from the map, and returns the corresponding value.

Args:
    args: The key of the value to pop, and an optional default value.

Returns:
    The value at the given key, or the default value if passed.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.keys
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the keys of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.values
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the values of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.items
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the key-value pairs of the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.clear
documentation: Removes all entries from the map.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.update
documentation:
Sets entries in the map from all entries in the passed `dict`.

Args:
    value: The `dict` from which to get the entries to update.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._map.Map.observe
documentation:
Subscribes a callback to be called with the map event.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the [MapEvent][pycrdt.MapEvent].
A collection used to store key-value entries in an unordered manner, similar to a Python `dict`.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._map.MapEvent
documentation:
A map change event.

Attributes:
    target (Map): The changed map.
    delta (list[dict[str, Any]]): A list of items describing the changes.
    path (list[int | str]): A list with the indices pointing to the map that was changed.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText.insert
documentation:
Inserts text at a given index, with optional attributes.

    value: The text to insert.
    attrs: The optional attributes.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText.insert_embed
documentation:
Insert an embed at a given index in the text, with optional attributes.

Args:
    index: The index at which to insert the embed.
    value: The embed to insert.
    attrs: The optional attributes.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText.format
documentation:
Formats existing text with attributes.

Args:
    start: The index at which to start applying the attributes (included).
    stop: The index at which to stop applying the attributes (excluded).
    attrs: The attributes to apply.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText.diff
documentation:
Returns:
    A list of formatted chunks that the current text corresponds to.
        Each list item is a tuple containing the chunk's contents and formatting attributes.
        The contents is usually the text as a string, but may be other data for embedded
        objects.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlText.clear
documentation: Removes the entire range of characters.

A piece of text in an XML tree.

This is similar to a [Text][pycrdt.Text], but instead of existing in a [Doc][pycrdt.Doc] on its
own, it is a child of [XmlElement][pycrdt.XmlElement] or [XmlFragment][pycrdt.XmlFragment].

A list-like view into an [XmlFragment][pycrdt.XmlFragment] or [XmlElement][pycrdt.XmlElement]'s
child nodes.

Supports `len`, `in`, and getting, setting, and deleting by index. Iteration will iterate over
key/value tuples.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.__len__
documentation:
Returns:
    The number of child nodes.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.__getitem__
documentation:
Args:
    index: The index of the child to get.

Raises:
    IndexError: Index out of bounds.

Returns:
    The child at the given index.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.__delitem__
documentation:
Args:
    key: The child index (`int`) or children `slice` to remove.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.__setitem__
documentation:
Replaces a child. Equivalent to deleting the index and then inserting the new value.

Args:
    key: The index of the child to replace.
    value: The new value at the index.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.__iter__
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over child nodes.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.insert
documentation:
Inserts a new node into the element's or fragment's children at the specified index.

Passing in a `str` will convert it to an `XmlText`. Returns the passed in element, which
will now be integrated into the tree.

Args:
    index: The index at which to insert the element.
    element: The element to insert.

Returns:
    The inserted element.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._xml.XmlChildrenView.append
documentation:
Appends a new node to the end of the element's or fragment's children.

Equivalent to `insert` at index `len(self)`.

Args:
    element: The element to append.

Returns:
    The appended element.

A list-like view into an [XmlFragment][pycrdt.XmlFragment] or [XmlElement][pycrdt.XmlElement]'s
child nodes.

Supports `iter`, `len`, and getting, setting, and deleting by index.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__init__
documentation:
Creates a text with an optional initial value:
```py
text = Text("Hello, World!")
```

Args:
    init: The string from which to initialize the text.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__iter__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("***")
for character in text:
    assert character == "*"
```

Returns:
    An iterable over the characters of the text.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__contains__
documentation:
Checks if the given string is in the text:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello, World!")
assert "World" in text
```

Args:
    item: The string to look for in the text.

Returns:
    True if the string was found.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__len__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello")
assert len(text) == 5
```

Returns:
    The length of the text.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__str__
documentation:
Returns:
    The text as a Python `str`.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.to_py
documentation:
Returns:
    The text as a Python `str`.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__iadd__
documentation:
Concatenates a string to the text:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello")
text += ", World!"
assert str(text) == "Hello, World!"
```

Args:
    value: The string to concatenate.

Returns:
    The concatenated text.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__delitem__
documentation:
Removes the characters at the given index or slice:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello, World!")
del text[5]
assert str(text) == "Hello World!"
del text[5:]
assert str(text) == "Hello"
```

Args:
    key: The index or the slice of the characters to remove.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Step not supported.
    RuntimeError: Negative start not supported.
    RuntimeError: Negative stop not supported.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the characters at the given index or slice:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello, World!")
assert text[:5] == "Hello"
```

Returns:
    The characters at the given index or slice.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.__setitem__
documentation:
Replaces the characters at the given index or slice with new characters:
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello, World!")
text[7:12] = "Brian"
assert text == "Hello, Brian!"
```

Args:
    key: The index or slice of the characters to replace.
    value: The new characters to set.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Step not supported.
    RuntimeError: Negative start not supported.
    RuntimeError: Negative stop not supported.
    RuntimeError: Single item assigned value must have a length of 1.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.clear
documentation: Remove the entire range of characters.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.insert
documentation:
Inserts a string at a given index in the text, with optional attributes.
```py
Doc()["text"] = text = Text("Hello World!")
text.insert(5, ",")
assert text == "Hello, World!"
```

Args:
    index: The index where to insert the string.
    value: The string to insert in the text.
    attrs: The formatting of attributes to apply.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.insert_embed
documentation:
Insert a value as an embed at a given index in the text, with optional attributes.

Args:
    index: The index where to insert the embedded content.
    value: The value to embed.
    attrs: The formatting attributes to apply.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.format
documentation:
Applies formatting attributes to a section of text between given start and stop indices.

Args:
    start: The index at which to start applying attributes (included).
    stop: The index at which to stop applying attributes (excluded).
    attrs: The formatting attributes to apply.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.diff
documentation:
Returns:
    A list of formatted chunks that the current text corresponds to.
        Each list item is a tuple containing the chunk's content and formatting attributes.
        The content is usually the text as a string, but may be other data for embedded
        objects.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._text.Text.observe
documentation:
Subscribes a callback to be called with the text event.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the [TextEvent][pycrdt.TextEvent].
A shared data type used for collaborative text editing, similar to a Python `str`.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._text.TextEvent
documentation:
A text change event.

Attributes:
    target (Text): The changed text.
    delta (list[dict[str, Any]]): A list of items describing the changes.
    path (list[int | str]): A list with the indices pointing to the text that was changed.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._update.get_state
documentation:
Returns a state from an update.

Args:
    update: The update from which to get the state.

Returns:
    The state corresponding to the update.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._update.get_update
documentation:
Returns an update consisting of all changes from a given update which have not
been seen in the given state.

Args:
    update: The update from which to get all missing changes in the given state.
    state: The state from which to get missing changes that are in the given update.

Returns:
    The changes from the given update not present in the given state.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._update.merge_updates
documentation:
Returns an update consisting of a combination of all given updates.

Args:
    updates: The updates to merge.

Returns:
    The merged updates.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    init: The initial root types of the document.
    client_id: An optional client ID for the document.
    allow_multithreading: Whether to allow the document to be used in different threads.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.guid
documentation: The GUID of the document.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.client_id
documentation: The document client ID.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.transaction
documentation:
Creates a new transaction or gets the current one, if any.
If an origin is passed and there is already an ongoing transaction,
the passed origin must be the same as the origin of the current transaction.

This method must be used with a context manager:

```py
with doc.transaction():
    ...
```

Args:
    origin: An optional origin to set on this transaction.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Nested transactions must have same origin as root transaction.

Returns:
    A new transaction or the current one.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.new_transaction
documentation:
Creates a new transaction.
Unlike [transaction()][pycrdt.Doc.transaction], this method will not reuse an ongoing
transaction.
If there is already an ongoing transaction, this method will wait (with an optional timeout)
until the current transaction has finished.
There are two ways to do so:

- Use an async context manager:
```py
async with doc.new_transaction():
    ...
```
In this case you most likely access the document in the same thread, which means that
the [Doc][pycrdt.Doc.__init__] can be created with `allow_multithreading=False`.

- Use a (sync) context manager:
```py
with doc.new_transaction():
    ...
```
In this case you want to use multithreading, as the ongoing transaction must
run in another thread (otherwise this will deadlock), which means that
the [Doc][pycrdt.Doc.__init__] must have been created with `allow_multithreading=True`.

Args:
    origin: An optional origin to set on this transaction.
    timeout: An optional timeout (in seconds) to acquire a new transaction.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Already in a transaction.
    TimeoutError: Could not acquire transaction.

Returns:
    A new transaction.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.get_state
documentation:
Returns:
    The current document state.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.get_update
documentation:
Args:
    state: The optional document state from which to get the update.

Returns:
    The update from the given document state (if any), or from the document creation.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.apply_update
documentation:
Args:
    update: The update to apply to the document.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.__setitem__
documentation:
Sets a document root type:
```py
doc["text"] = Text("Hello")
```

Args:
    key: The name of the root type.
    value: The root type.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Key must be of type string.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the document root type corresponding to the given key:
```py
text = doc["text"]
```

Args:
    key: The key of the root type to get.

Returns:
    The document root type.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.__iter__
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the keys of the document root types.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.get
documentation:
Gets the document root type corresponding to the given key.
If it already exists, it will be cast to the given type (if different),
otherwise a new root type is created.
```py
doc.get("text", type=Text)
```

Returns:
    The root type corresponding to the given key, cast to the given type.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.keys
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the names of the document root types.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.values
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the document root types.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.items
documentation:
Returns:
    An iterable over the key-value pairs of document root types.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.observe
documentation:
Subscribes a callback to be called with the document change event.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the [TransactionEvent][pycrdt.TransactionEvent].

Returns:
    The subscription that can be used to [unobserve()][pycrdt.Doc.unobserve].

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.observe_subdocs
documentation:
Subscribes a callback to be called with the document subdoc change event.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the [SubdocsEvent][pycrdt.SubdocsEvent].

Returns:
    The subscription that can be used to [unobserve()][pycrdt.Doc.unobserve].

methods.name: python.pycrdt._doc.Doc.unobserve
documentation:
Unsubscribes to changes using the given subscription.

Args:
    subscription: The subscription to unregister.

A shared document.

All shared types live within the scope of their corresponding documents.
All updates are generated on a per-document basis.
All operations on shared types happen in a transaction, whose lifetime is also bound to a
document.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    doc: The document the undo manager will work with.
    scopes: A list of shared types the undo manager will work with.
    capture_timeout_millis: A time interval for grouping changes that will be undone/redone.


Raises:
    RuntimeError: UndoManager must be created with doc or scopes.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.expand_scope
documentation:
Expands the scope of shared types for this undo manager.

Args:
    scope: The shared type to include.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.include_origin
documentation:
Extends the list of transactions origin tracked by this undo manager.

Args:
    origin: The origin to include.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.exclude_origin
documentation:
Removes a transaction origin from the list of origins tracked by this undo manager.

Args:
    origin: The origin to exclude.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.can_undo
documentation:
Returns:
    True if there are changes to undo.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.undo
documentation:
Perform an undo operation.

Returns:
    True if some changes were undone.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.can_redo
documentation:
Returns:
    True if there are changes to redo.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.redo
documentation:
Perform a redo operation.

Returns:
    True if some changes were redone.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.clear
documentation:
Clears all [StackItem][pycrdt.StackItem]s stored in this undo manager,
effectively resetting its state.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.undo_stack
documentation: The list of undoable actions.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._undo.UndoManager.redo_stack
documentation: The list of redoable actions.

The undo manager allows to perform undo/redo operations on shared types.
It can be initialized either with a [Doc][pycrdt.Doc] or with scopes.
Scopes are a list of shared types integrated in a document.
If initialized with a `Doc`, scopes can later be expanded.
Changes can be undone/redone by batches using time intervals.
It is possible to include/exclude changes by transaction origin in undo/redo operations.

type.name: Callable[[], None] | Callable[[Any], None] | Callable[[Any, ReadTransaction], None]
Callable[[], None] | Callable[[Any], None] | Callable[[Any, ReadTransaction], None]

documentation: Count the number of parameters in a callable

methods.name: python.pycrdt._base.BaseType.doc
documentation:
The document this shared type belongs to.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Not integrated in a document yet.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._base.BaseType.observe_deep
documentation:
Subscribes a callback for all events emitted by this and nested collaborative types.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the list of events.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._base.BaseType.unobserve
documentation:
Unsubscribes to changes using the given subscription.

Args:
    subscription: The subscription to unregister.

documentation:
Encodes a payload length.

Args:

Returns:
    The encoded payload length.

documentation:
Creates an [AWARENESS][pycrdt.YMessageType] message.

Args:
    data: The data to send in the message.

    The [AWARENESS][pycrdt.YMessageType] message.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.create_message
documentation:
Creates a SYNC message.

Args:
    data: The data to send in the message.
    msg_type: The [SYNC message type][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType].

Returns:
    The SYNC message.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.create_sync_step1_message
documentation:
Creates a [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType.SYNC_STEP1] message consisting
of a document state.

Args:

Returns:
    A [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType.SYNC_STEP1] message.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.create_sync_step2_message
documentation:
Creates a [SYNC_STEP2][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType.SYNC_STEP2] message in
reply to a [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType.SYNC_STEP1] message.

Args:

documentation:
Creates a [SYNC_UPDATE][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType] message that
contains a document update.

Args:

    A [SYNC_UPDATE][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType] message.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.read_message
documentation:
Reads a message from a byte stream.

Args:

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.write_message
documentation:
Writes a stream in a message.

Args:

Returns:

documentation:
Processes a [synchronization message][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType] on a document.

Args:
    message: A synchronization message.

functions.name: python.pycrdt._sync.create_sync_message

Creates a [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType] message that
contains the state of a [Doc][pycrdt.Doc].

Args:
    ydoc: The [Doc][pycrdt.Doc] for which to create the message.

Returns:
    A [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType] message.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._sync.YMessageType
documentation:
A generic Y message type.

Attributes:
    SYNC: A message type used for synchronizing documents.
    AWARENESS: A message type used for the awareness protocol.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._sync.YSyncMessageType
documentation:
A message type used for synchronizing documents.

Attributes:
    SYNC_STEP1: A synchronization message type used to send a document state.
    SYNC_STEP2: A synchronization message type used to reply to a
        [SYNC_STEP1][pycrdt.YSyncMessageType], consisting of all missing updates and all
        deletions.
    SYNC_UPDATE: A synchronization message type used to send document updates.

Encodes a number.

documentation:
Encodes a string.

Args:
    text: The string to encode.

    The binary stream.
An encoder capable of writing messages to a binary stream.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._sync.Decoder.read_var_uint

Decodes the current message length.

Returns:
    The decoded length of the message.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._sync.Decoder.read_message

Reads a message from the byte stream, ready to read the next message if any.

Returns:

A generator that reads messages from the byte stream.

A decoder capable of reading messages from a byte stream.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__init__
documentation:
Creates an array with an optional initial value:
```py
array0 = Array()
array1 = Array(["foo", 3, array0])
```

Args:
    init: The list from which to initialize the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__len__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array([2, 3, 0])
assert len(array) == 3
```

Returns:
    The length of the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.append
documentation:
Appends an item to the array.

Args:
    value: The item to append to the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.extend
documentation:
Extends the array with a list of items.

Args:
    value: The items that will extend the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.clear
documentation: Removes all items from the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.insert
documentation:
Inserts an item at a given index in the array.

Args:
    index: The index where to insert the item.
    object: The item to insert in the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.pop
documentation:
Removes the item at the given index from the array, and returns it.
If no index is passed, removes and returns the last item.

Args:
    index: The optional index of the item to pop.

Returns:
    The item at the given index, or the last item.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.move
documentation:
Moves an item in the array from a source index to a destination index.

Args:
    source_index: The index of the item to move.
    destination_index: The index where the item will be inserted.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__add__
documentation:
Extends the array with a list of items:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo"])
array += ["bar", "baz"]
assert array.to_py() == ["foo", "bar", "baz"]
```

Args:
    value: The items that will extend the array.

Returns:
    The extended array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__radd__
documentation:
Prepends a list of items to the array:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["bar", "baz"])
array = ["foo"] + array
assert array.to_py() == ["foo", "bar", "baz"]
```

Args:
    value: The list of items to prepend.

Returns:
    The prepended array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__setitem__
documentation:
Replaces the item at the given index with a new item:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo", "bar"])
array[1] = "baz"
assert array.to_py() == ["foo", "baz"]
```

Args:
    key: The index of the item to replace.
    value: The new item to set.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Index must be of type integer.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__delitem__
documentation:
Removes the item at the given index from the array:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo", "bar", "baz"])
del array[2]
assert array.to_py() == ["foo", "bar"]
```

Args:
    key: The index of the item to remove.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Array indices must be integers or slices.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the item at the given index:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo", "bar", "baz"])
assert array[1] == "bar"
```

Returns:
    The item at the given index.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__iter__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo", "foo"])
for value in array:
    assert value == "foo"
```
Returns:
    An iterable over the items of the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__contains__
documentation:
Checks if the given item is in the array:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array(["foo", "bar"])
assert "baz" not in array
```

Args:
    item: The item to look for in the array.

Returns:
    True if the item was found.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.__str__
documentation:
```py
Doc()["array"] = array = Array([2, 3, 0])
assert str(array) == "[2,3,0]"
```

Returns:
    The string representation of the array.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.to_py
documentation:
Recursively converts the array's items to Python objects, and
returns them in a list. If the array was not yet inserted in a document,
returns `None` if the array was not initialized.

Returns:
    The array recursively converted to Python objects, or `None`.

methods.name: python.pycrdt._array.Array.observe
documentation:
Subscribes a callback to be called with the array event.

Args:
    callback: The callback to call with the [ArrayEvent][pycrdt.ArrayEvent].
A collection used to store data in an indexed sequence structure, similar to a Python `list`.

classes.name: python.pycrdt._array.ArrayEvent
documentation:
An array change event.

Attributes:
    target (Array): The changed array.
    delta (list[dict[str, Any]]): A list of items describing the changes.
    path (list[int | str]): A list with the indices pointing to the array that was changed.
